anchor.snippet: Exponential growth occurs when a quantity grows as an exponential function of time
its instantaneous rate of change (that is, the derivative ) of a quantity with respect to an independent variable is proportional to the quantity itself
If the constant of proportionality is negative, then the quantity decreases over time, and is said to be undergoing exponential decay
In the case of a discrete domain of definition with equal intervals, it is also called geometric growth or geometric decay
